steps.title: Distribute both sides
steps.content:
Expand the equation:
$$10(15x-9)=-15(6-10x)$$
$$150x-90=-90+150x$$
The two sides are the same expression, just written in a different order.

steps.title: Recognize the identity
steps.content: Because $150x-90=150x-90$ is true for every value of $x$, the equation has infinitely many solutions. In Desmos, graph each side as a function; the two lines lie exactly on top of each other.

steps.title: Choose the answer
steps.content: The equation is not false and it is not limited to one or two values of $x$. It is true for all real $x$, so choice C is correct.
